result:
Implemented notebook Git sync in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go). There is now a `sync` command wired into `run()` and usage output. It treats `notes/` as its own Git repo, verifies the repo and upstream branch exist, stages all notebook changes, creates a `sync notebook <RFC3339 timestamp>` commit when needed, then runs `git pull --rebase` and `git push`. I added small Git helpers so command failures surface with clearer messages.

I also added tests in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go) for the main sync cases: commit/push to a local bare remote, pulling a remote change before pushing, and failing cleanly when `notes/` has no upstream configured. The README now documents the new command and the one-time Git setup flow in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md).

I couldn’t run `gofmt` or `go test` here because the sandbox does not have `gofmt` or `go` installed.
